name: http-integration-specialist
description: Specialized agent for managing HTTP communication between Angular frontend and .NET backend
scope: API integration, CORS policies, HTTP client configuration, SSL/TLS handling

rules.description: CORS is centrally configured in Program.cs, not per-controller
rules.whenToModify: Only when adding new origin, never modify per-endpoint

rules.description: Angular components must use absolute URL to backend: https://localhost:7001
rules.futureEnhancement: Extract to environment.ts for env-based configuration

rules.description: Backend runs on HTTPS localhost:7001, frontend on HTTP localhost:4200
rules.mixedContent: Browser allows HTTP frontend → HTTPS backend in localhost dev
rules.production: Ensure same protocol (HTTPS everywhere) in production

rules.description: Must be provided globally in app.config.ts via provideHttpClient()
Always provide error callback in subscribe to handle network failures
rules.common: 404 Not Found, 500 Server Error, Network timeout, CORS blocked

rules.description: No Bearer tokens, JWT, or auth headers currently implemented
rules.whenNeeded: Add HttpInterceptor in app.config.ts and services to inject auth headers

rules.description: Always declare response type in HttpClient methods for type safety
No timeout configured by default. Consider adding for production
rules.enhancement: Use timeout operator from RxJS: pipe(timeout(5000))

rules.description: ASP.NET Core automatically handles Content-Type: application/json
rules.noAction: Required, Angular HttpClient defaults to JSON

rules.onlyDev: Only enabled when IsDevelopment() is true
rules.useCase: Reference for API endpoints before implementation

basicApiCall.description: Basic pattern for calling backend API from Angular component

apiService.description: Dedicated service for API communication (best practice)

corsAddition.description: How to add a new allowed origin to CORS
steps: Add new origin URL to .WithOrigins() array

debuggingTools: Chrome DevTools Network tab for HTTP inspection

causes: Frontend origin not in CORS allowlist
corsError.solution: Verify origin in Program.cs CORS policy, restart backend

connectionRefused.solution: Ensure backend running: cd api/src && dotnet run

certificateError.solution: Https on localhost is normal in dev, browser will warn but still connect
